source.multipart_basis: Design-specific decomposition reflecting the CanSat primary-structure assembly called out in the original brief (CFRP shell, PA12 nose cap, PA12 base plate with parachute eyelet, two PCB-mount bulkheads) plus a recovery/electronics subdivision consistent with ESERO guideline integration flow. No rulebook citations fabricated.

full_prompt:
Design the primary structural assembly of a CanSat entry for ESA CanSat Europe 2025-26, compliant with the ESERO CanSat Competition Guidelines. The CanSat must fit within a cylindrical envelope of 115 mm height by 66 mm outer diameter; total wet mass must be exactly 350 g (-0/+10 g). The structure comprises a CFRP outer shell (wound 2-ply UD, nominal 0.8 mm wall), a 3D-printed PA12 top nose cap, a 3D-printed PA12 bottom base plate with parachute attachment eyelet, and two internal PCB-mount bulkheads at 30 mm and 75 mm from the base. Material properties: CFRP shell (E 70 GPa, tensile 600 MPa, density 1550 kg/m^3); PA12 (E 1.6 GPa, tensile 45 MPa, density 1010 kg/m^3). The CanSat deploys from a carrier rocket at approximately 1000 m AGL and descends under a parachute rated for minimum 1000 N deployment shock. Descent rate is 8-11 m/s per ESERO rulebook. Load cases: (LC1) launch qualification 20 g axial on 350 g wet mass (applied as distributed body force); (LC2) lateral qualification 5 g on 350 g; (LC3) parachute deployment shock: 1000 N axial at the base-plate eyelet (per rulebook minimum spec); (LC4) ground impact at 10 m/s descent rate: 15 g equivalent static deceleration on the base; (LC5) scrutineering pre-launch handling: 1 kg applied axially at the top nose cap (representing stack weight during integration). Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises stress (or composite Tsai-Wu index for CFRP) must remain below material-appropriate limits: CFRP < 300 MPa (600 / 2.0 FoS), PA12 < 22.5 MPa (45 / 2.0 FoS), across LC1-LC5; (R2) eyelet pull-out: local stress in PA12 near the eyelet insert under LC3 must remain below 22.5 MPa; (R3) structural dry mass (shell + nose + base + bulkheads only, excluding PCBs, battery, parachute) must not exceed 120 g; (R4) first natural frequency must exceed 80 Hz to avoid coupling with carrier rocket motor burn; (R5) envelope compliance: no point of the structure may exceed the 66 mm x 115 mm envelope under any static load case (rigid check). Thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling analyses not required for this basic structural benchmark. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly with shell, nose, base, two bulkheads as separate bodies; composite layup schedule; FEA report per load case; modal report; mass budget.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
Delivered as a multi-part assembly reflecting the physical build: an aeroshell split into top (nose/aeroshell-top) and bottom (base aeroshell), a payload deck that indexes electronics stacks, a parachute canister integrated into the nose volume, a recovery bay occupied by the two PCB bulkheads, a battery bracket tying the heaviest inertial mass to the primary structure, and an antenna mount on the aeroshell top. Interfaces are screw-fastened at the aeroshell split-line, the payload deck is captured between the two PCB bulkheads, the battery bracket is bolted to the lower bulkhead, and the parachute canister feeds loads into the base-plate eyelet. Final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC3 parachute shock). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structural assembly of a CanSat entry for ESA CanSat Europe 2025-26, compliant with the ESERO CanSat Competition Guidelines.

Envelope: cylindrical, 115 mm height x 66 mm outer diameter; total wet mass exactly 350 g (-0/+10 g). Structure: CFRP outer shell (wound 2-ply UD, nominal 0.8 mm wall), 3D-printed PA12 top nose cap, PA12 base plate with parachute attachment eyelet, two internal PCB-mount bulkheads at 30 mm and 75 mm from base.

Materials: CFRP shell (tensile 600 MPa); PA12 (tensile 45 MPa). CanSat deploys from carrier rocket at approximately 1000 m AGL; parachute rated for minimum 1000 N deployment shock; descent 8-11 m/s per ESERO rulebook.

Load cases: (LC1) launch qualification 20 g axial on 350 g wet mass; (LC2) lateral qualification 5 g on 350 g; (LC3) parachute deployment shock 1000 N axial at base-plate eyelet; (LC4) ground impact at 10 m/s: 15 g equivalent static deceleration on base; (LC5) scrutineering handling 1 kg applied axially at top nose cap.

Pass/fail: (R1) CFRP < 300 MPa (600/2.0 FoS), PA12 < 22.5 MPa (45/2.0 FoS), across LC1-LC5; (R2) eyelet pull-out PA12 stress under LC3 < 22.5 MPa; (R3) structural dry mass (shell + nose + base + bulkheads) <= 120 g; (R4) first natural frequency > 80 Hz; (R5) envelope compliance 66 mm x 115 mm under any static LC.

prompt.title: ESA CanSat Europe 2025-26 Primary Structure (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Compliant 66x115 mm 350 g CanSat with CFRP shell + PA12 nose/base, 20 g launch + 1000 N parachute shock. Decomposition: aeroshell top, aeroshell bottom, payload deck, parachute canister, recovery bay (two PCB bulkheads), battery bracket, antenna mount. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing load case.

parts.name: aeroshell_top
parts.role: upper CFRP aeroshell section integrating nose cap form and antenna mount boss
parts.material_hint: CFRP UD 2-ply co-cured with PA12 insert at nose cap ring

parts.name: aeroshell_bottom
parts.role: lower CFRP aeroshell section terminating at the PA12 base plate with parachute eyelet

geometric_constraints.eyelet_feature: through-thickness PA12 boss for parachute line

parts.material_hint: CFRP UD shell + PA12 SLS base plate (co-bonded)

parts.name: payload_deck
parts.role: PCB mount deck that indexes the primary electronics stack between the two bulkheads

parts.name: parachute_canister
parts.role: thin-walled canister that retains folded parachute within the upper volume and transfers 1000 N shock into the base eyelet via the recovery bay

parts.name: recovery_bay_bulkheads
parts.role: two PCB-mount bulkheads at 30 mm and 75 mm from base that form the recovery bay and carry parachute shock load path

parts.name: battery_bracket
parts.role: bracket retaining the battery (heaviest discrete inertial mass) against the lower bulkhead

parts.name: antenna_mount
parts.role: mast/boss on aeroshell top that positions the telemetry antenna clear of the CFRP shell

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: cylindrical; Z along longitudinal axis, Z=0 at base plate outer face

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC3 parachute shock).
Aeroshell split-line (IF1: A-B) separation under LC3 must remain below 0.2 mm to preserve envelope compliance.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: layered shell for CFRP; TET10 for PA12 nose/base and deck/bulkheads

interfaces.mediated_by: bonded/screwed aeroshell split-line at Z=75 mm (3x M2 radial)
interfaces.load_path: axial + shear load path between upper and lower aeroshell halves; carries LC1 launch and LC3 parachute shock

interfaces.mediated_by: screw-fastened PA12 bulkhead to CFRP shell inner wall via bonded inserts
interfaces.load_path: bulkheads react parachute shock from canister and launch inertia of battery/payload stack into shell

interfaces.mediated_by: payload deck captured between upper and lower bulkheads via 4x M2 spacers
interfaces.load_path: PCB stack inertial load transferred into bulkheads via the deck

interfaces.mediated_by: parachute canister bolted through upper bulkhead (E) into base-plate eyelet boss on B
interfaces.load_path: 1000 N parachute shock from canister -> upper bulkhead -> shell -> eyelet boss

interfaces.mediated_by: battery bracket bolted to lower bulkhead (2x M2)
interfaces.load_path: battery inertial mass under LC1/LC4 reacted through bracket into lower bulkhead

interfaces.mediated_by: antenna mount clamped into PA12 insert co-molded in aeroshell top
interfaces.load_path: local handling load (LC5) and aeroloads into aeroshell top

fem_check_policy.rationale: Parachute-shock load path (LC3) and envelope compliance (R5) are only meaningful on the assembled aeroshell + bulkheads + canister stack; eyelet pull-out (R2) requires the base-plate and lower bulkhead together; modal gate (R4) is an assembly-level frequency.

notes.why_multi_part: The brief explicitly enumerates CFRP shell, PA12 nose, PA12 base with eyelet, and two internal bulkheads as separate bodies; an integrated recovery canister, battery bracket, and antenna mount are standard CanSat subsystems and are captured here as distinct part families for assembly-level verification.
notes.speculation_caveats: parts[] counts and interfaces[] beyond the five explicitly-named bodies (shell, nose, base, two bulkheads) are design speculation consistent with the brief; no new rulebook text is invented.
